{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded={{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}} | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ({{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}}) by the decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Sanjō|ja|後三条天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Sanjō|qq=}}, upon the petition of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}}, who brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukubusuma Shrine|qq=}} * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|ja|真宗大谷派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded={{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}} | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ({{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}}) by the decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Sanjō|ja|後三条天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Sanjō|qq=}}, upon the petition of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}}, who brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukubusuma Shrine|qq=}} * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|ja|真宗大谷派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ({{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}}) by the decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Sanjō|ja|後三条天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Sanjō|qq=}}, upon the petition of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}}, who brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukubusuma Shrine|qq=}} * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|ja|真宗大谷派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded={{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}} | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ({{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}}) by the decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Sanjō|ja|後三条天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Sanjō|qq=}}, upon the petition of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}}, who brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukubusuma Shrine|qq=}} * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded={{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}} | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ({{ill|1069|ja|1069th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}}) by the decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Sanjō|ja|後三条天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Sanjō|qq=}}, upon the petition of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}}, who brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukubusuma Shrine|qq=}} * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|ja|真宗大谷派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded={{ill|1069|ja|1069年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}} | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama|qq=}}, Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ({{ill|1069|ja|1069年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1069|qq=}}) by the decree of {{ill|Emperor Go-Sanjō|ja|後三条天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Sanjō|qq=}}, upon the petition of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}}, who brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=}} decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: {{ill|Tsukubusuma Shrine|ja|都久夫須麻神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukubusuma Shrine|qq=}} * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Station|ja|長浜駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Station|qq=}} == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū|ja|浄土真宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū|qq=}} {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|ja|真宗大谷派|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha|qq=}} {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded=[[:en:1069|1069]] | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded=[[:en:1069|1069]] | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * [http://www.biwa.ne.jp/~hatimang/ Hikiyama festival Nagahama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shiga}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{citation needed|ja|阿賀神社|version=102029703|comment=Imported full ja revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | founded=[[:en:1069|1069]] | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * [http://www.biwa.ne.jp/~hatimang/ Hikiyama festival Nagahama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shiga}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{citation needed|ja|阿賀神社|version=102029703|comment=Imported full ja During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded=[[:en:1069|1069]] | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舎那院}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舎那院}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded=[[:en:1069|1069]] | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舎那院}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=}} == External links == {{Commons|Category:Nagahama-hachimangu}} * [http://www.biwa.ne.jp/~hatimang/ Hikiyama festival Nagahama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Shiga}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{citation needed|ja|阿賀神社|version=102029703|comment=Imported full ja It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=|1=Nagahama Hikiyama Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653306}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=|1=Burg Nagahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q621281}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舎那院|1=Shana-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613521}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=|1=Shana-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613521}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q179971}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=|1=Shana-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613521}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=|1=Daitsū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583061}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=|1=Kurokabe square|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678068}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=|1=Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653322}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11653283]] Wikidata name: Nagahama Hachiman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nagahama Hachimangu | location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town 13-55 | type_deg=35|Rank_min=22|Rank_sec=57.5|N/S=N | longitude_deg=136|longitude_min=16|longitude_sec=27.6|E/W=E | map_country_code=JP | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded=[[:en:1069|1069]] | Main_hall=Shinmei-zukuri | Pilgrimage={{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|ja|神仏霊場巡拝の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qq=|1=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444949}} No. 137 (Shiga No. 5) | annual_festival={{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} (April 12-17) | Rituals= | image=[[File:Nagahama hachimangu02s3200.jpg|250px]]
Main hall | map=Japan Shiga }} [[File:Nagahama hachimangu01s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Worship hall]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu04s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Tsukubusuma Shrine, auxiliary shrine]] [[File:Nagahama hachimangu06s3200.jpg|thumb|224px|Stone lanterns on the approach]] '''Nagahama Hachimangu''' (長浜八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Miyamae Town. It is a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. It is famous for the {{ill|Nagahama Hikiyama festival|ja|長浜曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Hikiyama festival|qq=|1=Nagahama Hikiyama Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653306}}, one of the {{ill|Three Great Float festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大一覧#祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Float festivals of Japan|qq=|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. == History == According to tradition, it was founded in the late Heian period ([[:en:1069|1069]]) by the decree of [[:en:Emperor Go-Sanjō|Emperor Go-Sanjō]], upon the petition of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], who brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, it fell into decline due to war, but was restored by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] when he became the lord of {{ill|Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|ja|長浜城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Castle (Ōmi Province)|qq=|1=Burg Nagahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q621281}}. Since the Heian period, the temple {{ill|Shinhōjū-ji|ja|新放生寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinhōjū-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舎那院|1=Shana-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613521}} existed within the grounds as the temple attached to the shrine, but it was abolished during the early Meiji period under the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]] decree, and all statues and documents were transferred to its remaining sub-temple, {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=|1=Shana-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613521}}. == Deities == * East Hall - {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|足仲彦尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q179971}} * Middle Hall - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} * West Hall - {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|息長足姫尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}} [[ja:長浜八幡宮]] == Grounds == * Worship hall * Main hall * Auxiliary shrine: Kōra Shrine * Auxiliary shrine: [[:en:Tsukubusuma Shrine|Tsukubusuma Shrine]] * Auxiliary shrine: Tenmangū * Auxiliary shrine: Konpiragū * Subordinate shrine: Suehiro Inari Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Jishu Shrine * Subordinate shrine: Kumano Shrine * Wish-fulfilling arrow == Events and festivals == * {{ill|Hikiyama festival|ja|曳山祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikiyama festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山車|1=matsuri float|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470974}} – April 12-17. A traditional event dating back to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. One of the Three Great Float festivals of Japan. == Access == * 13-minute walk from JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]] [[:en:Nagahama Station|Nagahama Station]] == Nearby Information == * {{ill|Shana-in|ja|舎那院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shana-in|qq=|1=Shana-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613521}} * [[:en:Jōdo Shinshū|Jōdo Shinshū]] [[:en:Ōtani-ha|Jōdo Shinshū Ōtani-ha]] {{ill|Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|ja|真宗大谷派長浜別院大通寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Betsuin Daitsu-ji|qq=|1=Daitsū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583061}} * {{ill|Kurokabe Square|ja|黒壁スクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokabe Square|qq=|1=Kurokabe square|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678068}} * {{ill|Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|ja|長浜赤十字病院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|qq=|1=Nagahama Red Cross Hospital|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653322}}
